about.label: Start Your Project
about.heading: A team built
about.subheading: Kinetic started with a simple frustration: too many remodels feel rushed, disconnected, or over-promised. We built our process around the opposite.

features.title: Design before demolition
features.description: We map materials, sequencing, and scope in full before any work begins — no surprises once the crew arrives.

features.title: One point of contact
features.description: You're not chasing down subcontractors or guessing at status — one person owns your project from first call to final walkthrough.

features.title: Craft that outlasts the crew
features.description: We treat every home like it's the one we're going to live in — protected, respected, and finished right.

card.label: OUR EXPERIENCE
card.subStat: Years creating beautiful kitchens, baths, and homes.

items.title: Kitchen Remodeling
items.description: New layouts, cabinetry, and finishes built around how your household actually cooks, gathers, and lives day to day.
items.subtitle: Transform your kitchen into a beautiful, functional space that fits your lifestyle and budget.

beforeAfter.afterAlt: Renovated kitchen with new cabinets and countertops
beforeAfter.title: Kitchen Transformation
beforeAfter.description: Complete kitchen remodel with custom cabinets, quartz countertops, and modern appliances.

beforeAfter.afterAlt: Open, updated galley kitchen after renovation
beforeAfter.title: Galley Kitchen Refresh
beforeAfter.description: Widened sightlines, new lighting, and updated finishes turned a cramped galley layout into an open, functional space.

steps.title: In-Home Consultation & Measure
steps.description: We walk your kitchen with you, listen to how you actually use the space, and take precise measurements before anything is designed.

steps.title: Design & Material Selection
steps.description: Layout, cabinetry, countertops, and finishes are locked in together, with a fixed-price estimate before any demolition begins.

steps.title: Demolition & Construction
steps.description: Our crew handles demo, plumbing, electrical, and installation in sequence, with daily site communication so you always know what stage you're at.

steps.title: Final Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk every inch of the finished kitchen with you and don't consider the job done until you've signed off.

faqs.question: How long does a kitchen remodel typically take?
faqs.answer: Most kitchen remodels take four to six weeks from demolition to final walkthrough, depending on the scope of layout changes and custom cabinetry lead times.

faqs.question: Can I still use my kitchen during the remodel?
faqs.answer: Not fully — once plumbing and electrical work begins, the kitchen is unusable. We help set up a temporary cooking area elsewhere in your home for the duration.

faqs.question: Do you handle the plumbing and electrical work?
faqs.answer: Yes, our crew coordinates all trades — plumbing, electrical, and installation — under one point of contact, so nothing falls through the cracks between subcontractors.

faqs.question: Will I get a fixed price before work begins?
faqs.answer: Yes. Layout, cabinetry, countertops, and finishes are locked in together with a fixed-price estimate before any demolition starts.

items.title: Bathroom Remodeling
items.description: From a simple refresh to a full gut renovation — fixtures, tile, and storage chosen to fit your space and routine.
items.subtitle: Create a luxurious retreat with expert bathroom renovation services tailored to your needs.

beforeAfter.afterAlt: Renovated bathroom with walk-in shower and new vanity
beforeAfter.title: Bathroom Makeover
beforeAfter.description: Luxurious bathroom renovation with walk-in shower, vanity upgrade, and premium fixtures.

beforeAfter.beforeAlt: Small, dated powder room before remodel
beforeAfter.title: Powder Room Refresh
beforeAfter.description: New tile, fixtures, and lighting brought a small, dated powder room fully up to date.

steps.title: In-Home Consultation
steps.description: We assess plumbing, layout, and any moisture or structural issues on-site before proposing a scope.

steps.title: Design & Fixture Selection
steps.description: Tile, vanity, fixtures, and layout are finalized together, with a clear written estimate before work starts.

steps.title: Demolition & Installation
steps.description: Plumbing, waterproofing, tile, and fixtures are installed in the right order to avoid the callbacks that rushed bathroom work usually causes.

steps.title: Final Quality Check
steps.description: We test every fixture and seal before handoff, so the first time you use the space is also the last time anyone touches it.

faqs.question: How long does a bathroom remodel take?
faqs.answer: A typical bathroom renovation takes two to three weeks, depending on whether plumbing fixture locations are changing.

faqs.question: Do you offer emergency bathroom repairs?
faqs.answer: We focus on planned renovations rather than emergency repairs, but we're happy to prioritize scheduling if a failing fixture is part of your remodel scope.

faqs.question: What's the most impactful upgrade in a bathroom remodel?
faqs.answer: Rethinking the shower — a curbless entry and rain showerhead consistently change how a bathroom feels more than any surface finish choice.

faqs.question: Will you handle waterproofing?
faqs.answer: Yes — proper waterproofing behind tile and around fixtures is done before a single tile goes down, since that's what prevents costly leaks later.

items.title: Whole-Home Remodeling
items.description: Additions, floor-plan changes, and full-home renovations coordinated under one team from first sketch to final walkthrough.
items.subtitle: Complete home transformation services that breathe new life into every corner of your property.

beforeAfter.title: Whole-Home Transformation
beforeAfter.description: Full-property renovation spanning kitchen, living spaces, and structural layout changes.

beforeAfter.title: Open-Concept Conversion
beforeAfter.description: Removing non-structural walls turned a closed-off, room-by-room layout into one connected open-concept living space.

steps.title: Discovery & Scope
steps.description: We walk the entire property, define a clear scope room by room, and set realistic expectations before any numbers are put on paper.

steps.title: Design & Permitting
steps.description: Full architectural and material decisions are locked in and permits are filed before demolition — so decisions get made at the drawing board, not on the job site.

steps.title: Phased Construction
steps.description: One crew, one point of contact manages scheduling, subcontractors, and daily communication across every phase of the build.

steps.title: Final Walkthrough & Handoff
steps.description: We don't consider the project done until you've walked every room and signed off — your home, finished right.

faqs.question: Can I stay in my home during a whole-home remodel?
faqs.answer: Often yes, if the work is phased room by room — we plan sequencing specifically to keep part of the home livable throughout construction.

faqs.question: How far in advance should materials be selected?
faqs.answer: Before demolition begins. Locking in finishes early is what keeps a whole-home project on schedule and on budget.

faqs.question: Do you handle permits for a full remodel?
faqs.answer: Yes — permitting and inspections are coordinated as part of our project management, filed before demolition starts.

faqs.question: How long does a whole-home remodel take?
faqs.answer: Typically three to six months, depending on square footage and how many rooms are affected.

items.title: Home Additions
items.description: Expand your living space with thoughtfully designed additions that blend seamlessly with your home's architecture and meet your family's growing needs.
items.subtitle: Expand your living space with seamless home additions that blend perfectly with your existing structure.

beforeAfter.afterAlt: Home exterior with new room addition
beforeAfter.title: Seamless Room Addition
beforeAfter.description: A new living space added to the existing structure, matched to the home's original architecture.

beforeAfter.title: Second-Story Addition
beforeAfter.description: A full second-story build-out added bedrooms and living space without expanding the home's existing footprint.

steps.title: Site Assessment & Feasibility
steps.description: We evaluate your lot, foundation, and local zoning constraints to confirm what's actually buildable before designing anything.

steps.title: Design & Permit Approval
steps.description: Structural and architectural plans are finalized and submitted for permit approval before ground is broken.

steps.title: Foundation & Framing
steps.description: Foundation, framing, and the building envelope go up first, with regular inspections at each structural milestone.

steps.title: Final Finishes & Walkthrough
steps.description: Interior finishes are completed and the new space is walked room by room with you before we call it done.

faqs.question: Do home additions require a full architectural redesign?
faqs.answer: Not always — additions that build on an existing footprint or match the current roofline often need less structural rework than a from-scratch design.

faqs.question: Will an addition increase my home's resale value?
faqs.answer: Generally yes, especially additions that add bedrooms or bathrooms, though the return depends heavily on how well it matches the home's existing style.

faqs.question: How long does the permit process take for an addition?
faqs.answer: Permit approval typically takes four to eight weeks depending on your local jurisdiction, which we factor into your overall project timeline.

faqs.question: Can you build up instead of out?
faqs.answer: Yes — a second-story addition over an existing footprint preserves yard space entirely, though it requires a closer look at your home's existing structure first.

items.description: Built-to-order cabinets and storage solutions crafted to maximize space, improve organization, and complement your home's unique style.
items.subtitle: Handcrafted custom cabinets designed to maximize storage and enhance your home's aesthetic.

beforeAfter.afterAlt: Home office after custom built-in shelving
beforeAfter.title: Built-In Shelving

steps.title: Design Consultation & Measurements
steps.description: We measure your space and talk through storage needs, style, and budget before drawing anything up.

steps.title: Material & Finish Selection
steps.description: Wood species, hardware, and finish are chosen together, with a fixed quote before fabrication begins.

steps.title: Fabrication
steps.description: Cabinetry is built to your exact measurements in our shop, not adjusted on-site after the fact.

steps.title: Delivery & Installation
steps.description: Finished cabinetry is delivered and installed by the same team that measured it, so fit is never a guessing game.

faqs.question: Is custom cabinetry more durable than stock cabinetry?
faqs.answer: Custom cabinetry is typically built with better joinery and hardware, so it tends to hold up longer, especially in high-use kitchens.

faqs.question: How long does custom cabinetry take to build?
faqs.answer: Usually six to ten weeks from final design approval, depending on wood species and finish complexity.

faqs.question: Can you match existing cabinetry in my home?
faqs.answer: In most cases yes — we can match wood species, stain, and hardware style to blend new cabinetry with what's already there.

faqs.question: Do you install the cabinetry yourselves?
faqs.answer: Yes — the same team that measures your space also delivers and installs the finished cabinetry, so fit is never a guessing game.

faqs.question: What flooring holds up best with pets?
faqs.answer: Luxury vinyl plank and engineered hardwood with a higher-durability finish tend to resist scratches better than solid hardwood.

faqs.question: Is it worth mixing flooring materials room to room?
faqs.answer: Often yes — matching material to a room's actual use (moisture, foot traffic, comfort) usually beats forcing one material through the whole home.

faqs.question: How long does flooring installation take?
faqs.answer: Most flooring projects take one to two weeks, depending on square footage and whether old flooring needs to be removed first.

faqs.question: Do you repair the subfloor if needed?
faqs.answer: Yes — proper subfloor prep happens before any plank or tile goes down, since that's what actually determines how long a new floor lasts.

blogs.label: Journal
blogs.heading: Notes from
blogs.subheading: Short reflections on materials, light, rooms, and the small choices that make a space feel calm and complete.

items.title: Small Kitchen Remodel Ideas for Seattle Homes
items.description: A look at rooms that feel open, calm, and easy to live in without losing warmth.
items.author: Awais
items.content:
Renovating a small kitchen requires careful planning to maximize every square inch. Here in Seattle, where natural light can be scarce during winter, design choices matter even more.

Maximize Natural Light

Using reflective surfaces and keeping window treatments minimal can significantly bounce light around the room. Consider glossy tile backsplashes or lighter cabinetry to keep the space feeling airy.

Smart Storage Solutions

Consider taking cabinets all the way to the ceiling to capture unused vertical space. Built-in organizers for your drawers and pull-out pantry shelves are essential for keeping clutter off the countertops.

The Appliance Garage

A dedicated cabinet to hide your coffee maker, toaster, and blender can visually clean up a small kitchen instantly.

faqs.question: How small is too small for a kitchen remodel?
faqs.answer: Even galley kitchens under 70 square feet can be transformed with the right layout — the limiting factor is usually storage planning, not floor area.

faqs.question: Does a small kitchen remodel cost less than a large one?
faqs.answer: Generally yes, since less material and labor are needed, though per-square-foot costs can run higher for custom storage solutions.

items.title: Designing a Spa-Style Bathroom at Home
items.description: Simple material and layout choices that turn an ordinary bathroom into a daily retreat.
items.imageAlt: Warm, minimal bathroom with natural stone
items.author: Awais
items.content:
A spa-style bathroom isn't about square footage — it's about slowing down the details most homeowners rush past: water flow, material texture, and light temperature.

Choosing Calming Materials

Honed stone, matte tile, and warm wood-look vanities read as quieter than glossy, high-contrast finishes. Texture does more work here than color.

Water as an Experience

A rain showerhead and a freestanding tub change how a bathroom feels to use, not just how it looks in photos.

faqs.question: What's the most impactful upgrade in a bathroom remodel?
faqs.answer: Rethinking the shower — a curbless entry and rain showerhead consistently change how a bathroom feels more than any surface finish.

faqs.question: How long does a typical bathroom remodel take?
faqs.answer: Two to three weeks for a full renovation, depending on whether plumbing locations are changing.

items.title: Planning a Whole-Home Remodel Without the Chaos
items.description: How sequencing and a single point of contact keep a full-home renovation from unraveling mid-project.
items.author: Awais
items.content:
Whole-home remodels fail for the same reason most big projects do: decisions get made in the wrong order. Getting sequencing right up front prevents most of the surprises that derail a renovation.

Design Before Demolition

Every material, fixture, and layout decision should be locked in before a wall comes down. Changing your mind mid-project is where budgets and timelines both slip.

One Team, One Timeline

Coordinating trades under a single project lead avoids the finger-pointing that happens when subcontractors are scheduled independently.

Living Through It

Phasing work room by room, rather than opening the whole house at once, keeps at least part of your home livable throughout.

faqs.question: Can I stay in my home during a whole-home remodel?
faqs.answer: Often yes, if the work is phased room by room — we plan sequencing specifically to keep part of the home livable.

faqs.question: How far in advance should materials be selected?
faqs.answer: Before demolition begins. Locking in finishes early is what keeps a whole-home project on schedule.

items.title: Adding a Room Without Losing Your Yard
items.description: How thoughtful additions expand living space while keeping a home's outdoor footprint intact.
items.imageAlt: Home addition blending into an existing structure
items.author: Awais
items.content:
Most homeowners assume an addition means giving up half the backyard. In practice, the right footprint and orientation can add significant square footage while barely touching outdoor space.

Building Up Instead of Out

A second-story addition over an existing footprint preserves yard space entirely, though it requires a closer look at your home's existing structure.

Matching the Original Architecture

Rooflines, siding, and window proportions should echo the existing home so the addition reads as original rather than bolted on.

faqs.question: Do home additions require a full architectural redesign?
faqs.answer: Not always — additions that build on an existing footprint or match the current roofline often need less structural rework than a from-scratch design.

faqs.question: Will an addition increase my home's resale value?
faqs.answer: Generally yes, especially additions that add bedrooms or bathrooms, though the return depends heavily on how well it matches the home's existing style.

items.title: Textures of Stillness
items.description: How layered materials can add quiet depth to kitchens, baths, and living spaces.
items.author: Awais
items.content:
A quiet room isn't just about acoustics; it's about visual noise. Layering materials thoughtfully creates a sense of deep calm that painted drywall alone cannot achieve.

The Role of Natural Wood

Incorporating natural wood tones brings immediate warmth to cold spaces. Whether it's floating white oak shelves or a custom walnut vanity, organic grain patterns ground the room.

Matte vs. Gloss Finishes

We prefer matte finishes for their ability to absorb light softly, reducing glare and promoting stillness. Plaster walls, honed stone counters, and brushed brass fixtures all contribute to this muted, relaxed aesthetic.

faqs.question: Is custom cabinetry more durable than stock cabinetry?
faqs.answer: Custom cabinetry is typically built with better joinery and hardware, so it tends to hold up longer, especially in high-use kitchens.

faqs.question: How long does custom cabinetry take to build?
faqs.answer: Usually six to ten weeks from final design approval, depending on wood species and finish complexity.

items.title: Choosing the Right Floor for Every Room
items.description: Matching flooring material to how a room actually gets used, not just how it looks in a showroom.
items.imageAlt: Wide-plank hardwood flooring in a sunlit room
items.author: Awais
items.content:
The flooring that looks best in a showroom isn't always the right choice for how a specific room in your home actually gets used day to day.

Durability Where It Matters

Entryways and kitchens see the most wear — luxury vinyl and engineered hardwood hold up better here than softer solid wood.

Warmth Underfoot

Bedrooms and living rooms benefit from materials that feel warm to bare feet, which is where wide-plank hardwood or wool carpet earn their keep.

Moisture-Prone Rooms

Bathrooms and laundry rooms need genuinely water-resistant materials — tile and waterproof vinyl remain the safest choices.

faqs.question: What flooring holds up best with pets?
faqs.answer: Luxury vinyl plank and engineered hardwood with a higher-durability finish tend to resist scratches better than solid hardwood.

faqs.question: Is it worth mixing flooring materials room to room?
faqs.answer: Often yes — matching material to a room's actual use (moisture, foot traffic, comfort) usually beats forcing one material through the whole home.

items.title: Light as a Material
items.description: Using daylight to shape mood, highlight finishes, and guide movement through a home.
items.author: Awais
items.content:
We often think of materials as things we can touch—stone, wood, metal. But daylight is perhaps the most transformative material in your home, completely altering how architectural elements are perceived throughout the day.

Direct and Indirect Lighting

Combining recessed lighting with ambient sconces creates layers of illumination. Indirect light scraping across a textured wall highlights the craftsmanship in a way flat lighting never could.

Shadows Matter

Don't be afraid of shadows. They provide depth and contrast, anchoring your furniture to the room and defining spaces without the need for physical walls.

faqs.question: What happens in a design consultation?
faqs.answer: We walk your space, talk through how you actually use each room, and map lighting and material direction before any drawings begin.

faqs.question: Is a design consultation free?
faqs.answer: Yes — the initial consultation is complimentary and has no obligation to move forward.

items.title: The Beauty of Restraint
items.description: Why fewer, more deliberate choices — and clear planning behind them — create the most memorable spaces.
items.imageAlt: Minimal interior with warm architectural lines
items.author: Awais
items.content:
In modern design, the temptation to add 'just one more thing' is strong. Restraint is the discipline of knowing when to stop — and that discipline starts with planning, not decorating.

Curating Your Palette Early

Sticking to three primary materials or colors, decided before construction starts, prevents visual clutter and costly mid-project changes.

Negative Space

Leaving walls blank or surfaces empty allows the architecture of your home to breathe. It turns the items you do display into focal points rather than background noise.

faqs.question: How far in advance should a project be planned?
faqs.answer: Ideally all material and scope decisions are finalized before demolition — that's what keeps a restrained, deliberate design intact through construction.

faqs.question: Does planning add time to a project?
faqs.answer: It adds time up front but typically shortens the build itself by avoiding decisions made (and reversed) mid-project.

whyChooseUs.label: Why Choose Us
whyChooseUs.heading: Clarity, not
whyChooseUs.subheading: Remodeling has a reputation for surprise costs and vanishing contractors. We run ours differently — clear numbers up front, one team from start to finish, and a finished space that feels intentional, not improvised.

faq.label: FAQs
faq.heading: Common questions,
faq.subheading: Straightforward answers about our process, pricing, and what it's actually like to work with Kinetic — the questions we hear most, answered before you have to ask.

items.question: How do I get started with a project?
items.answer: It starts with a free consultation — we walk the space, talk through your goals and budget, and define a clear scope before anything goes on paper. From there we move into design and a written estimate, so you know exactly what to expect before work begins.

items.question: How are your estimates put together?
items.answer: Every estimate is delivered in writing after we've finalized materials, scope, and sequencing together — no verbal ballparks, no surprise change orders once the crew is on site.

items.question: How long does a typical remodel take?
items.answer: It depends on scope — a bathroom refresh might take two to three weeks, while a whole-home remodel can run several months. We'll walk you through a realistic timeline during planning, and our five-step process is built to avoid the delays that usually come from decisions made mid-project.

items.question: Will I have one point of contact throughout the project?
items.answer: Yes. One person owns your project from the first call to the final walkthrough — you're never left chasing down subcontractors or guessing who to call with a question.

items.question: How quickly do you respond to inquiries?
items.answer: We aim to respond to every inquiry within 24 hours, usually sooner. You can also reach us directly through the contact section below.

items.question: Do you handle permits and inspections?
items.answer: Yes — permitting and inspections are coordinated as part of our project management, handled alongside the rest of your build so you don't have to manage it separately.

testimonials.label: Client Stories
testimonials.heading: Don't just take
testimonials.subheading: Real results, real homeowners. Every remodel we finish leaves a story — these are a few of them told in the clients' own words.

items.body: A thorough review was done of our home's outdated kitchen — new layout options presented, materials sourced on our behalf, and progress updates at every stage. The stress we expected simply never arrived.
items.author: Margaret Holt

items.body: We've worked with Kinetic on two projects and the experience has been consistent every time. They explain every decision clearly, keep to the timeline, and the finished results speak entirely for themselves.
items.author: Daniel Reeves

items.body: From first consultation to final walkthrough, the team was professional, honest, and genuinely invested in our home. The bathroom renovation exceeded what we'd imagined — we couldn't be more pleased.
items.author: Claire Ashton

items.body: Transparent pricing, no surprise costs, and a crew that treated our home with real care. This is how remodeling should be done — clear from the first call to the last nail.
items.author: Tom & Susan Barker

trustHero.heading: Remodeling You Can
trustHero.description: We provide expert kitchen, bath, and whole-home remodeling designed to keep your project on time, on budget, and built to last. From first sketch to final walkthrough, our team makes the whole process feel effortless.

kitchen-remodeling.heading: Reliable Kitchen Remodeling
kitchen-remodeling.description: At Meridian, we specialize in fast, reliable, and beautifully finished kitchen remodels for homes and businesses alike.
kitchen-remodeling.missionText: Whether it's a full gut renovation or a targeted cabinet-and-countertop refresh, we make the process smooth, fast, and stress-free from start to finish.

bathroom-remodeling.heading: Reliable Bathroom Remodeling
bathroom-remodeling.description: At Meridian, we specialize in fast, reliable, and beautifully finished bathroom renovations for every budget and space.
bathroom-remodeling.missionText: Whether it's a full gut renovation or a simple fixture refresh, we make the process smooth, fast, and stress-free from start to finish.

whole-home-remodeling.heading: Reliable Whole-Home Remodeling
whole-home-remodeling.description: At Meridian, we specialize in coordinated, full-property renovations that touch every corner of your home without the usual chaos.
whole-home-remodeling.missionText: Whether it's a single-room refresh or a top-to-bottom transformation, we make the process smooth, fast, and stress-free from start to finish.

home-additions.heading: Reliable Home Additions
home-additions.description: At Meridian, we specialize in seamless home additions that expand your living space without disrupting your day-to-day.
home-additions.missionText: Whether it's a single new room or a full second story, we make the process smooth, fast, and stress-free from start to finish.

custom-cabinetry.heading: Reliable Custom Cabinetry
custom-cabinetry.description: At Meridian, we specialize in handcrafted, built-to-order cabinetry designed around exactly how you use your space.
custom-cabinetry.missionText: Whether it's a single built-in or cabinetry for a full home, we make the process smooth, fast, and stress-free from start to finish.

flooring.heading: Reliable Flooring Installation
flooring.description: At Meridian, we specialize in fast, reliable, and durable flooring installation for homes and businesses alike.
flooring.missionText: Whether it's a single room or your whole home, we make the process smooth, fast, and stress-free from start to finish.

design-consultation.heading: Reliable Design Guidance
design-consultation.description: At Meridian, we specialize in clear, honest design consultations that turn vague ideas into a real, actionable plan.
design-consultation.missionText: Whether you're just exploring options or ready to move forward, we make the process smooth, fast, and stress-free from start to finish.

project-planning.heading: Reliable Project Planning
project-planning.description: At Meridian, we specialize in detailed, transparent project planning that keeps every renovation organized from day one.
project-planning.missionText: Whether it's a small project or a major renovation, we make the process smooth, fast, and stress-free from start to finish.
